perturbation.original_text: 1.16 "Gross Revenue" means any and all revenue actually received and collected by a Party, or any Subsidiary or other Affiliate, licensee, distributor or reseller thereof, from (i) any SkypeOut, SkypeIn or Skype Plus services (or other products or services similar to Skype Plus developed under this Agreement by the Parties) provided through or in connection with the Company-Skype Branded Application, Company-Skype Branded Content or Company-Skype Branded Web Site, and (ii) any advertising, licensing, distribution or other exploitation of the Company-Skype Branded Application, Company-Skype Branded Content or Company-Skype Branded Web Site. For purposes of clarity, save as otherwise provided in this Agreement, no development (including, without limitation, any development, updating, upgrading and improvement of software or other technology), sales, promotional, operating, customer support or marketing costs incurred by any Party, including, without limitation, of any third party, in connection with the Company- Skype Branded Application, Company-Skype Branded Content or Company-Skype Branded Web Site, shall be deducted from Gross Revenue.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.16 "Gross Revenue" means revenue collected by a Party from services related to the Company-Skype Branded Application, Company-Skype Branded Content or Company-Skype Branded Web Site.  For purposes of clarity, save as otherwise provided in this Agreement, development costs incurred by any Party in connection with the Company- Skype Branded Application, Company-Skype Branded Content or Company-Skype Branded Web Site, may be deducted from Gross Revenue.
perturbation.explanation: The modification to the definition of "Gross Revenue" introduces an ambiguity regarding which revenue streams are included. By removing "any and all" and making it a simple "revenue" the definition is made less strict, and removing the text specifying what type of revenue is considered 'gross revenue' leaves room to interpretation for what should and shouldn't be included. Furthermore, the modification now permits the deduction of development costs from Gross Revenue, contradicting the original explicit prohibition of such deductions, this is an omission and creates an In Text Contradiction. This change creates an in-text contradiction and uncertainty in financial reporting and revenue sharing calculations, potentially affecting payments outlined in Section 5.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1 Other Revenue. The Company-Skype Branded Application will permit Company-Skype Branded Customers to access basic features at no charge to the Company-Skype Branded Customers. It is hereby acknowledged and agreed that the rights granted by the Online Group hereunder are not exclusive (save as provided herein) and the Online Group will offer its own products and services through various channels and may receive subscription and other fees ("Separate Fees") for such services ("Separate Services"), other than and not in any manner relating to the Company-Skype Branded Application, the Company-Skype Toolbar, the Company-Skype Branded Web Site or the Company-Skype Branded Content, offered by the Online Group from time to time and accessible separate from, and entirely independent of, the Company-Skype Branded Application, the Company-Skype Toolbar, Company-Skype Branded Web Site and/or the Company-Skype Branded Content. No portion of the Separate Fees shall be payable to or shared in by Skype or the Group. Notwithstanding the foregoing, if approved in writing by Skype, which approval may be withheld in its sole discretion, the Online Group may (i) charge any additional or separate fees to access the Company-Skype Branded Web Site, Company-Skype Branded Content, Company-Skype Branded Application or Company-Skype Toolbar, or (ii) offer any Separate Services or charge any Separate Fees by or through the Company-Skype Branded Web Site, Company-Skype Branded Content, Company-Skype Branded Application or Company-Skype Toolbar. It is hereby acknowledged and agreed that the rights granted by Skype hereunder are not exclusive (save as provided herein) and Skype will offer its own products and services, other than and not in any manner relating to the Company- Skype Branded Application, Company-Skype Toolbar, Company-Skype Branded Web Site or Company-Skype Branded Content, through various channels accessible separate from, and entirely independent of, the Company-Skype Branded Application, Company-Skype Toolbar, Company- Skype Branded Web Site and/or the Company-Skype Branded Content, and that no portion of the revenue derived from such products and services shall be payable to or shared with the Online Group or the Group.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by altering the definition of 'Gross Revenue.' Originally, 'Gross Revenue' explicitly disallowed deductions for development costs. The changed text now permits the deduction of development costs, directly conflicting with the original definition. This creates an ambiguity regarding which revenue streams are included and allows for deductions previously prohibited, impacting revenue calculations that may affect payments outlined elsewhere in the agreement, such as in Section 5.1, which discusses revenue sharing. The omission of 'any and all' further weakens the definition.

perturbation.original_text: 5.4 Audit Rights. The Parties (including Online BVI on behalf of the Online Group) agree to maintain records (i) of all information reasonably necessary to verify all calculations to be made under Section 8.3.2, and (ii) supporting, verifying and necessary to demonstrate the calculation and collection of fees and/or revenue, as well as any deductions thereto, and payments made hereunder, including, without limitation, budgets, purchase orders, expense records, invoices, correspondence, banking and financial and other records pertaining to the determination of Gross Revenue, Direct Expenses and Adjusted Net Revenue, during the term of this Agreement and for a period of two (2) years following the expiration or termination hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.4 Audit Rights. The Parties (including Online BVI on behalf of the Online Group) agree to maintain records (i) of all information reasonably necessary to verify all calculations to be made under Section 8.3.2, and (ii) supporting, verifying and necessary to demonstrate the calculation and collection of fees and/or revenue, as well as any deductions thereto, and payments made hereunder, during the term of this Agreement and for a period of two (2) years following the expiration or termination hereof.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting "including, without limitation, budgets, purchase orders, expense records, invoices, correspondence, banking and financial and other records pertaining to the determination of Gross Revenue, Direct Expenses and Adjusted Net Revenue" from the section on Audit Rights, the scope of documentation required for audits is significantly narrowed. While the parties are still expected to maintain records, the specific types of records are no longer explicitly listed. This creates an In-Text Contradiction with Section 5.3.1 which requires detailed statements showing Gross Revenue, Direct Expenses and Adjusted Net Revenue. The omission undermines the ability to verify the accuracy of these statements, leading to potential disputes and enforcement challenges. This change affects the verifiability of financial obligations outlined in Section 5.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.3.1 Within thirty (30) days of the end of each calendar quarter during the Term, the Company will, and Online BVI will cause the Company to, furnish to Skype complete and accurate statements (each, a "Statement") in a form acceptable to Skype, certified to be accurate by an officer of the Company or Online BVI, as applicable, showing with reasonable detail (i) the Gross Revenue actually received and collected by the Company or Online BVI, or if applicable, any Subsidiary or other Affiliate (including, without limitation, a Subsidiary or other Affiliate of Tom Holding), licensee, distributor or reseller thereof, during the preceding calendar quarter and on a cumulative basis, (ii) the Direct Expenses incurred thereby, during the preceding calendar quarter and on a cumulative basis, and (iii) the calculation of Adjusted Net Revenue. In the event that Skype provides any Payment Services hereunder, within thirty (30) days of the end of each calendar quarter during the Term, Skype shall furnish to the Company complete and accurate Statements in a form acceptable to Online BVI, certified to be accurate by an officer of Skype, showing with reasonable detail (i) the Gross Revenue actually received and collected by Skype, or if applicable, its Subsidiaries or other Affiliates (including, without limitation, a Subsidiary or other Affiliate of Skype Holding), licensee, distributor or reseller thereof, during the preceding calendar quarter and on a cumulative basis, (ii) the Direct Expenses incurred thereby, during the preceding calendar quarter and on a cumulative basis, and (iii) the calculation of Adjusted Net Revenue. The respective Parties shall use their best efforts to accompany each respective Statement with payment, to Skype, if provided by the Company or Online BVI (or their associated third parties set forth above), or to the Company (or Online BVI, as applicable), if provided by Skype (or its associated third parties set forth above), as applicable, of 50% of the Adjusted Net Revenue shown on the respective Statements. Provided that the respective Parties utilize their best efforts to provide such payments together with the respective Statements, no Party shall be deemed in breach hereof for delivering late payment until the date which is sixty (60) days following the last day of the respective applicable calendar quarter. All payments shall be paid in Euros, and in the event revenue is earned by a Party (or its associated third parties set forth above) in currencies other than in Euros, then such Party shall convert said amounts each month into Euros based upon the exchange rate published by the Wall Street Journal as of the fifteenth day of such month or if such day shall fall on a non-business day then as of the first business day following said fifteenth day.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 5.4 listed specific types of records (budgets, purchase orders, etc.) that must be maintained for audit purposes. The changed text omits this list, narrowing the scope of documentation required. This directly contradicts Section 5.3.1, which mandates 'complete and accurate statements showing with reasonable detail' Gross Revenue, Direct Expenses, and Adjusted Net Revenue. Without the specified records in Section 5.4, verifying the accuracy of the statements in Section 5.3.1 becomes significantly more difficult, creating a strong in-text contradiction that undermines the enforceability and verifiability of the financial obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 13.1 Company Indemnification. Tom Holding unconditionally guarantees the timely performance of all of the obligations of Online BVI, the Group and the Online Group hereunder, and agrees to defend, indemnify, and hold harmless Skype, Skype Holding, their affiliated companies and Subsidiaries and their respective officers, directors, employees and agents from and against any loss, claim, cost, expense, liability or damage, including reasonable attorney's fees and costs resulting from a third-party claim that directly arises from: (i) a claim that the Group Rights infringe the intellectual property or other proprietary rights of any third party; (ii) a breach of the Online Group's and the Group's representations and warranties hereunder made; (iii) the performance of the Online Group's and the Group's obligations hereunder; or (iv) the Online Group's and the Group's, or their respective employees' negligence, misrepresentations or other tortious, illegal or unauthorized conduct in the promotion of the Company-Skype Branded Application or any other act or omission arising out of or relating to this Agreement. Such indemnification obligation of Tom Holding is conditioned upon Skype promptly notifying Tom Holding in writing setting forth with specificity the claim or action to which such indemnification obligation applies, and reasonable cooperation, information, and assistance in connection therewith. Tom Holding will have the right to control the defense of each such claim and any lawsuit or proceeding arising therefrom. In no event will Skype settle any such claim or lawsuit or proceeding arising therefrom without the prior written approval of Tom Holding. In defending against such claim or action, Tom Holding may (i) contest; (ii) settle; (iii) and in the case of any claim that the Group Rights infringe the intellectual property or other proprietary rights of a third party, (a) procure for Skype and its customers the right to continue using the Group Rights, as applicable, or (b) modify or replace the Group Rights, as applicable, so that it they longer infringe.
perturbation.changed_text: 13.1 Company Indemnification. Tom Holding guarantees the obligations of Online BVI and the Group hereunder and agrees to defend, indemnify, and hold harmless Skype, their affiliated companies and their respective officers, directors, employees and agents from and against any loss, claim, cost, expense, liability or damage resulting from a third-party claim that arises from: (i) a claim that the Group Rights infringe the intellectual property rights of any third party; (ii) a breach of the Online Group's and the Group's representations and warranties hereunder made. Such indemnification obligation of Tom Holding is conditioned upon Skype notifying Tom Holding in writing setting forth the claim or action to which such indemnification obligation applies. Tom Holding will have the right to control the defense of each such claim and any lawsuit or proceeding arising therefrom. In defending against such claim or action, Tom Holding may (i) contest; (ii) settle;
perturbation.explanation: Several omissions are introduced in this section: 1.  The removal of "unconditionally guarantees the timely performance of all of the obligations of ... the Online Group", weakening the scope of the guarantee provided by Tom Holding. 2. The phrase "including reasonable attorney's fees and costs" is removed limiting the guaranteed coverage. 3. The removal of clauses (iii) and (iv) relating to the performance of obligations and negligence, misrepresentations, or tortious conduct, substantially limiting the events that trigger indemnification. 4. The omission of the need for 'specificity', and 'reasonable cooperation, information and assistance'. 5. The omission of the condition that 'Skype need written approval from Tom Holding in the event of settling'. 6. And the removal of clause (iii) where Tom Holding can also 'procure, modify, or replace the Group Rights'. By omitting these points, and particularly the point of needing specificity, an in-text contradiction is created with Section 13.4, and undermining a claim for indemnification. This could hinder Skype's ability to seek full legal recourse and complicates the resolution of disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13.4 Notice; Participation. The party claiming indemnification pursuant to this Section 13 ("Indemnified Party") shall promptly notify the other party ("Indemnifying Party") of any such claim of which it becomes aware and shall: (i) at the Indemnifying Party's expense, provide reasonable cooperation to the Indemnifying Party in connection with the defense or settlement of any such claim, and (ii) at the Indemnified Party's expense, be entitled to participate in the defense of any such claim.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the scope of Tom Holding's indemnification obligations, removing the requirement for specificity in the notice provided by Skype, as well as the cooperation. This directly contradicts section 13.4, which states that the Indemnified Party (Skype) shall provide reasonable cooperation to the Indemnifying Party (Tom Holding) and notify of any such claim of which it becomes aware. The removal of the specificity clause in the changed text undermines Tom Holding's ability to properly defend itself as envisioned in 13.4, creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by Omissions - In Text Contradiction.
